data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) vs child B3 (dc58: completion-verifier as an engine workflow, uses -> prompt -> uses -> uses)] Clean — no findings.

4-bucket audit of parent acceptance/success criteria against this child, with whole-roster absence cross-check:

- Parent AC2 (verify_completion runs as an engine workflow using existing step kinds only — uses->prompt(agentic)->uses->uses; no new constructs): FULLY covered by this child. B3 AC1/AC2 author the v3 completion-verification.yaml expressing precheck (uses) -> branch -> agentic verify (prompt) -> reconcile (uses), wrapping the existing completion.py helpers (child-closure+signature precheck, FAIL<=>findings reconcile, citation downgrade) with no logic change; AC3/AC4 make "without behavioural change" observable via golden-fixture parity + failure-path/invariant tests; AC5 validates against the v3 schema + linter. (B3 expresses the tail as a branch — uses -> branch(then: prompt->uses, else: passthrough) — to faithfully model completion.py's short-circuit, and defers signing to B5 since completion.py has no signing helper; these are shape/consistency questions for G4, not G3 coverage gaps.)

- Parent Success Criterion, "completion-verification runs on the ONE workflow-engine substrate (its prompts in the engine prompt library)" half: PARTIALLY covered by this child (B3 PRODUCES the workflow offline as expand-contract/additive; the LIVE cutover that makes it the running close-gate is B5/f12d by design — B3's Why/Scope state the existing completion.py path stays the live gate, cutover/removal is B5). Not an uncovered-criterion finding: the remainder is covered by the named sibling B5.

Criteria covered by named siblings (structural / not-this-child — each cross-checked present in the roster, so NOT a finding):
- Parent AC1 (plan-review as engine workflow + production batch-runner from sizing.py): B2 (6b06, closed) + B1 (723c).
- Parent AC3 (planned-trace parity harness, offline FakeRunner, CI): B4 (101a, closed) — depends on B2+B3 and asserts parity for BOTH gates incl. completion-verification.
- Parent AC4 (bespoke path cut over behind the gate once parity holds; close/claim gates keep working, signed verdicts/attestations intact): B5 (f12d, closed) + 0408 — B3 explicitly defers cutover + signing here.
- Parent AC5 (throwaway samples removed; shims/public API preserved; epic A demo retained): B6 (7e48, closed).
- Parent AC6 (end state: exactly two real workflows + one sample; make lint + full suite pass): B6 (7e48). B3's own AC6 (make lint + full suite pass) contributes its own deliverable's green-suite obligation.

Conclusion: every parent acceptance/success criterion is either fully covered by this child (AC2) or fully covered by a named sibling already present in the roster (AC1/B2+B1, AC3/B4, AC4/B5+0408, AC5/B6, AC6/B6); the completion-verification-on-substrate half of the SC is partly delivered by this child and completed at live cutover by B5. No parent criterion is left uncovered, and no uncovered-criterion finding is attributable to this pairing. No findings.
